reasoning: The question asks if the US will publicly accuse Russia of invading another country (other than Ukraine) before July 1, 2025. Background text establishes the context of Putin 'going for' another country next. Extensive searching did not uncover any events between April 2024 and July 1, 2025, in which Russia sent ground forces into a new country, nor did it find the U.S. government making any accusations of such an invasion. Speculation about Moldova, the Baltics, or Georgia remains just that—speculation. There was an August 2024 recognition by a US Embassy of the anniversary of the 2008 Georgia invasion, but this does not constitute a new accusation of invasion in the spirit (or typical resolution practice) of questions framed around 'what country will Putin go for next.' There are no unexpected edge cases, making a negative resolution virtually certain.
